title: Electrical and electronics technicians
description: Apply electrical and electronic theory and related knowledge, usually under the direction of engineering staff, to design, build, repair, adjust, and modify electrical components, circuitry, controls, and machinery for subsequent evaluation and use by engineering staff in making engineering design decisions. Operate, test, maintain, or adjust unmanned, automated, servomechanical, or electromechanical equipment. May operate unmanned submarines, aircraft, or other equipment to observe or record visual information at sites such as oil rigs, crop fields, buildings, or for similar infrastructure, deep ocean exploration, or hazardous waste removal. May assist engineers in testing and designing robotics equipment. Build, install, test, or maintain robotic equipment or related automated production systems. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Observe sets or locations for potential problems and to determine filming and lighting requirements, and confer with directors, sound and lighting technicians, electricians, and other crew members to discuss assignments and determine filming sequences, desired effects, camera movements, and lighting requirements.

Adjust positions and controls of cameras, printers, and related equipment to change focus, exposure, and lighting, and use cameras in different mounts while operating zoom lenses and composing shots according to specifications.
Operate television or motion picture cameras to record scenes for broadcasts, and observe monitors to determine audio and video levels while controlling audio equipment during productions.
Design and modify equipment to employer specifications, and set up and operate portable field transmission equipment outside the studio while monitoring and logging transmitter readings.
Diagnose and resolve media system problems, including reporting equipment issues and maintaining programming logs as required by management and regulatory bodies.

Install, adjust, and operate electronic equipment for recording, editing, and transmitting programs, and perform maintenance or minor repairs, while supervising the installation of equipment and systems.
Record and edit audio material, synchronize and equalize sounds with visual actions, and mix and edit voices, music, and sound effects for various productions.
Prepare for recording sessions by selecting and setting up microphones, and regulate sound quality during sessions using control consoles.
Monitor and log transmitter performance and incoming and outgoing signals, while adjusting equipment to maintain broadcast quality.
Assemble electrical systems or prototypes and fabricate parts, using hand tools or measuring instruments according to engineering instructions and industry standards.
Test, clean, maintain, and repair electrical and electronic equipment, and identify and resolve malfunctions by consulting manuals and schematics.
Coordinate work with engineers and technicians during production, and document testing or operation of equipment by maintaining logs or manuals.
Educate equipment operators on proper equipment use, and do regular preventive maintenance tasks to avert potential equipment problems.
Compile and maintain records documenting engineering schematics and equipment installations, and review engineering criteria to ensure compliance and necessary revisions.
Install electrical and electronic components and systems based on documentation, and perform scheduled maintenance and repairs of those systems.
Maintain inventories of spare parts for all equipment and consult with engineers to procure necessary replacements and ensure optimal equipment functioning.
Research equipment needs, sources, competitive pricing, and operational costs to inform decision making and project specifications.
Develop training materials or manuals to assist with the knowledge transfer regarding equipment operation and maintenance.
Stay current with new technologies in the field and analyze industry advancements by reading trade material.
Inspect and test electrical or electronic systems to locate and diagnose malfunctions, using visual inspections, testing instruments, and computer software.
Install equipment and accessories, such as stereos, navigation equipment, communication equipment, and security systems, as well as fixtures, outlets, terminal boards, and switches using hand tools.
Estimate costs of repairs based on parts and labor charges, and complete repair bills, shop records, or expense reports.
Confer with customers to determine the nature of malfunctions and provide advice regarding equipment operation, maintenance, or programming.
Run new speaker and electrical cables; cut openings and drill holes for fixtures and equipment, using electric drills and routers.
Replace and clean electrical or electronic components and assist engineers in designing, configuring, or applying robotic systems.
Record results of diagnostic tests and maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs.
Repair, adjust, or replace electrical or mechanical components or parts, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering equipment, and reassemble machines after making repairs.
Test components or circuits of faulty equipment to locate defects, using oscilloscopes, signal generators, and other diagnostic software programs.
Align, adjust, or calibrate equipment according to specifications and enter information into computers to copy programs or modify schematics.
Consult with customers, supervisors, or engineers to plan layout of equipment or to resolve problems in system operation or maintenance.
Maintain parts inventories and order any additional parts needed for repairs and maintain inventories of robotic production supplies.
Operate machines to test the functioning of parts or mechanisms and conduct performance tests on electromechanical assemblies.
Develop or modify industrial electronic devices, circuits, or equipment, according to available specifications, and produce related technical drawings using CAD software.
Make repairs to robotic devices or peripheral equipment, such as replacement of defective circuit boards, and perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems.
Align, fit, or assemble component parts using hand or power tools, fixtures, templates, or microscopes and inspect components for accurate assembly or defects.
Train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots and train new repairers.
Monitor tool and part inventories and inspect and monitor work areas to ensure safety and compliance.
Install new robotic systems in stationary positions or on tracks, and build or assemble robotic devices or systems.
Identify equipment performance issues through statistical analysis and equipment performance records, and troubleshoot robotic systems using knowledge of various electronic components.
Maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems and update existing equipment, performing tasks such as installing updated circuit boards.

Confer with personnel, such as management, engineering, quality control, customer, or union workers' representatives, to coordinate work activities, resolve employee grievances, or identify and review resource needs, and recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, discharges, or disciplinary measures.
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work.

Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations, using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses or shears, or welding equipment, and inspect, test, and measure completed work, using devices such as hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures and develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or computer information management systems.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as time and production records, inventory data, repair or maintenance statistics, or test results.
Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate drones for aerial videography and photography during live events or for pre-recorded material.
Operate drones to capture aerial or unique angle footage for film, television, or video productions.
Perform installation, testing, adjustment, and repair of avionics equipment in uncrewed aerial vehicles, such as drones.
Use drones for inspection of high-voltage lines and other hard-to-reach equipment.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
